# REQ-QR-260010: Reusable UI Component Library and Vertical-Slice Delivery Convention

- Requirement ID: QR-260010
- Class: QR (Quality attribute — maintainability and extensibility)
- Status: Accepted
- Date: 2026-06-09
- Linked ADR: ADR-260010
- Linked backlog item: BI-260010

## Statement

The public site codebase must provide a set of composable Astro component templates and a
`src/features/` vertical-slice convention such that a new page or feature can be built without
duplicating markup, styles, or layout patterns from existing pages.

## Rationale

Current duplication — three identical `.policy`/`.meta`/`.trademark`/`code` style blocks across
`privacy.astro`, `terms.astro`, and `google-addon/support.astro`; no reusable UI component
baseline beyond `SignInForm.astro` — violates the maintainability acceptance check in Section 6
of `project-instructions.md`.

## Measurable Acceptance Signals

- Zero duplicated `.policy`/`.meta`/`.trademark`/`code` style blocks across page files.
- At least four foundational UI component templates exist under `src/components/ui/` and are each
  consumed by at least one page.
- `src/features/` folder exists with a documented vertical-slice convention.
- All existing behavior tests (52 unit tests, 38 e2e tests) continue to pass after the change.
